frontmatter.name: calendar-weekly-planner
frontmatter.description: Plan your week by reviewing upcoming events, suggesting time blocks, and helping prioritize tasks

marketplace.summary: Review calendar and plan your week with smart suggestions

calendar-weekly-planner.description_html: Plan your week by reviewing upcoming events, suggesting time blocks, and helping prioritize tasks
calendar-weekly-planner.body_html:
Calendar Weekly Planner

Plan your week effectively by reviewing upcoming events, blocking focus time, and prioritizing what matters most.

Features

  • Week overview — See all meetings, deadlines, and commitments at a glance
  • Focus time blocks — Suggest uninterrupted work periods based on your schedule
  • Task prioritization — Help rank tasks by urgency and importance
  • Conflict detection — Identify overlapping commitments or unrealistic workloads
  • Prep reminders — Alert you to prepare for upcoming meetings

How It Works

  1. Review — Scan your calendar for the upcoming week
  2. Analyze — Identify busy periods, gaps, and potential conflicts
  3. Suggest — Recommend time blocks for deep work, breaks, and prep
  4. Plan — Help you commit to a realistic weekly plan

Example Conversation

User: "Help me plan next week" Myah: "Looking at your calendar, you have 12 meetings scheduled and 3 major deadlines. I recommend:

  • Monday: Block 9-11am for deep work on the Q4 report
  • Tuesday: Your busiest day — 6 meetings. Schedule a 30-min buffer at 2pm
  • Wednesday: Light meeting day — perfect for the presentation prep
  • Thursday: Deadline day — keep morning clear for final reviews
  • Friday: 3 meetings, wrap up week — suggest ending at 3pm

Would you like me to add these focus blocks to your calendar?"

User: "What's my busiest day?" Myah: "Tuesday is your busiest with 6 meetings back-to-back from 10am to 5pm. I strongly recommend adding a lunch break and a 15-min buffer between the 2pm and 3pm meetings."

Tips

  • Review your plan Sunday evening or Monday morning
  • Be realistic about what you can accomplish
  • Protect your focus time aggressively
  • Build in buffers for unexpected issues

frontmatter.name: daily-standup
frontmatter.description: Generate daily standup reports from your activity, calendar, and task list

marketplace.summary: Auto-generate daily standup updates from your work activity

daily-standup.description_html: Generate daily standup reports from your activity, calendar, and task list
daily-standup.body_html:
Daily Standup

Generate your daily standup report automatically from your calendar, completed tasks, and ongoing work.

Features

  • Yesterday's accomplishments — Pull from your completed tasks and calendar
  • Today's plan — Review upcoming meetings and scheduled work
  • Blockers — Identify potential issues or dependencies
  • Team-friendly format — Structured for Slack, email, or standup tools
  • Weekly rollup — Aggregate daily updates into a weekly summary

How It Works

  1. Connect your data — Calendar, task manager, code commits (optional)
  2. Generate — Create your standup report each morning
  3. Customize — Adjust tone, detail level, and format
  4. Share — Copy to Slack, email, or your standup tool

Example Conversation

User: "Generate my standup report" Myah: "Here's your standup update for today:

Yesterday:

  • Completed API integration for user authentication
  • Reviewed 3 pull requests from the team
  • Fixed bug in payment processing flow
  • Attended product planning meeting (notes shared)

Today:

  • Finish writing tests for authentication API
  • Start on password reset feature
  • 1:1 with Sarah at 2pm
  • Team standup at 10am

Blockers:

  • Waiting for design mockups from the design team (expected Thursday)
  • Staging environment is down — IT is working on it

Would you like me to adjust anything or format this for a specific platform?"

User: "Format it for Slack" Myah: "Here's your Slack-formatted standup:

*Yesterday:*
:white_check_mark: Completed API integration for user authentication
:white_check_mark: Reviewed 3 pull requests from the team
:white_check_mark: Fixed bug in payment processing flow
:white_check_mark: Attended product planning meeting (notes shared)

*Today:*
:arrow_right: Finish writing tests for authentication API
:arrow_right: Start on password reset feature
:arrow_right: 1:1 with Sarah at 2pm
:arrow_right: Team standup at 10am

*Blockers:*
:warning: Waiting for design mockups from the design team (expected Thursday)
:warning: Staging environment is down — IT is working on it

Just copy and paste into Slack!"

Customization Options

  • Detail level — Brief (3 bullets) vs. Detailed (full sentences)
  • Tone — Casual (emojis) vs. Professional (formal)
  • Focus areas — Development, meetings, research, etc.
  • Include/exclude — Choose what data sources to pull from

Tips

  • Generate your standup at the same time each day for consistency
  • Review and edit before sharing — automation helps, but you know best
  • Use the weekly rollup for 1:1s or progress reviews
  • Flag blockers early so they can be addressed quickly

frontmatter.name: honcho-memory-inspector
frontmatter.description: Inspect and manage your Honcho memory store — view peers, conclusions, and memory insights

marketplace.summary: View and manage your AI memory and insights

honcho-memory-inspector.description_html: Inspect and manage your Honcho memory store — view peers, conclusions, and memory insights
honcho-memory-inspector.body_html:
Honcho Memory Inspector

This skill lets you inspect and understand what Myah remembers about you through the Honcho memory system.

What You Can Do

  • View memory peers — See what topics and concepts Myah has learned about you
  • Review conclusions — Understand the insights Myah has drawn from your conversations
  • Manage memory — Request deletion or updates to specific memories
  • Export insights — Get a summary of your memory profile

Commands

"What do you know about me?" Shows a summary of your memory profile, including key topics and insights.

"Show my memory peers" Lists all the peer categories in your Honcho memory store.

"What conclusions have you drawn?" Displays recent conclusions and insights from your conversations.

"Delete memory about [topic]" Removes specific memories from your store (requires confirmation).

Privacy Notes

  • Your memory data is private and encrypted
  • You have full control over what is remembered
  • Memories improve Myah's ability to help you
  • You can request a complete memory export or deletion at any time

Example Conversation

User: "What do you remember about my work style?" Myah: "Based on our conversations, I've learned that you prefer detailed morning briefings, you work best with structured task lists, and you often need help prioritizing competing deadlines. You also prefer concise email drafts over verbose ones."

User: "Delete everything about my personal preferences" Myah: "I'll remove all memory entries related to your personal preferences. This will take effect immediately. Are you sure you want to proceed?"

frontmatter.name: inbox-zero
frontmatter.description: Process emails efficiently with smart sorting, draft replies, and inbox management strategies

marketplace.summary: Triage emails, draft replies, and achieve inbox zero

inbox-zero.description_html: Process emails efficiently with smart sorting, draft replies, and inbox management strategies
inbox-zero.body_html:
Inbox Zero

Achieve and maintain inbox zero with intelligent email triage, smart reply drafting, and effective email management strategies.

Features

  • Smart triage — Categorize emails by urgency and action required
  • Draft replies — Generate context-aware response drafts
  • Follow-up tracking — Identify emails needing follow-up and schedule reminders
  • Newsletter digest — Summarize newsletters and bulk emails
  • Unsubscribe suggestions — Identify mailing lists you rarely open

Workflow

  1. Scan — Review new emails and categorize them
  2. Triage — Sort into: Reply Now, Reply Later, Delegate, Archive, Delete
  3. Draft — Create reply drafts for urgent items
  4. Schedule — Set follow-up reminders for pending items
  5. Clear — Archive or delete processed emails

Example Conversation

User: "Help me process my inbox" Myah: "You have 23 unread emails. Here's my triage:

Reply Now (3):

  • Client meeting request for tomorrow — needs confirmation
  • Team member asking about the project deadline
  • Invoice requiring approval

Reply Later (5):

  • Industry newsletter — I'll summarize key points
  • Project update — review after lunch
  • Meeting notes — no immediate action needed

Archive (12):

  • Notifications, updates, completed threads

Delete (3):

  • Spam, expired offers

Shall I draft replies for the urgent items?"

User: "Draft a reply to the client meeting request" Myah: "Here's a draft:

Hi [Client Name],

Thank you for reaching out. I'm available tomorrow at 2pm or 4pm EST. Would either of those work for you?

Looking forward to catching up.

Best regards

Would you like me to adjust the tone or suggest different times?"

Strategies

  • The 2-minute rule — If it takes less than 2 minutes, do it now
  • Batch processing — Handle emails in dedicated blocks, not continuously
  • Template library — Save common replies for faster responses
  • Scheduled sends — Draft now, send at optimal times

frontmatter.name: meeting-follow-up
frontmatter.description: Generate meeting summaries, action items, and follow-up emails from meeting notes or transcripts

marketplace.summary: Summarize meetings and track action items automatically

meeting-follow-up.description_html: Generate meeting summaries, action items, and follow-up emails from meeting notes or transcripts
meeting-follow-up.body_html:
Meeting Follow-Up

Never lose track of what was decided in meetings. This skill generates summaries, extracts action items, and drafts follow-up communications.

Features

  • Meeting summaries — Concise recap of key discussion points
  • Action item extraction — Identify who does what by when
  • Follow-up emails — Draft summary emails to attendees
  • Decision logging — Track important decisions made
  • Meeting prep — Review previous meeting notes before the next one

How to Use

Share your meeting notes, transcript, or a brief description of what was discussed. I'll help you organize and follow up.

Example Conversation

User: "We just had a product planning meeting. Here's what we discussed..." Myah: "Thanks for the update. Here's a structured summary:

Meeting Summary: Product Planning

Date: Today Attendees: [List from your notes]

Key Decisions

  1. Q2 Feature Priority — Mobile app redesign takes precedence over web updates
  2. Budget Allocation — $50K approved for user research
  3. Timeline — Beta launch moved from June to July

Action Items

  • @Sarah — Finalize user research vendor selection (Due: Friday)
  • @Mike — Create mobile redesign mockups (Due: Next Wednesday)
  • @You — Update project timeline and communicate to stakeholders (Due: Tomorrow)

Follow-Up Needed

  • Schedule architecture review meeting
  • Confirm vendor contracts

Would you like me to draft a follow-up email to the team?"

User: "Yes, draft the follow-up email" Myah: "Here's a draft:

Subject: Product Planning Meeting — Summary & Action Items

Hi Team,

Thanks for a productive planning session today. Here's a summary of what we covered:

Decisions Made:

  • Mobile app redesign is our Q2 priority
  • $50K budget approved for user research
  • Beta launch timeline adjusted to July

Action Items:

  • Sarah: Finalize research vendor by Friday
  • Mike: Mobile mockups by next Wednesday
  • [Your name]: Update timeline and communicate to stakeholders by tomorrow

Next Steps:

  • Architecture review meeting to be scheduled
  • Vendor contracts pending confirmation

Please reply to confirm your action items or if I missed anything.

Best

Shall I send this or would you like to edit it first?"

Tips

  • Share notes immediately after meetings while memory is fresh
  • Include attendee names for accurate action item assignment
  • Review previous meeting summaries before the next session
  • Set reminders for action item due dates

frontmatter.name: myah-welcome
frontmatter.description: Onboarding skill that introduces new users to Myah's capabilities and helps them get started

marketplace.summary: Guide new users through Myah setup and first steps

myah-welcome.description_html: Onboarding skill that introduces new users to Myah's capabilities and helps them get started
myah-welcome.body_html:
Myah Welcome

Welcome to Myah! I'm your personal AI business management assistant. This skill helps you get started and discover everything Myah can do for you.

What Myah Can Do

  • Manage your business communications — emails, meetings, follow-ups
  • Organize your documents — receipts, invoices, research
  • Plan your week — calendar management, task prioritization
  • Analyze data — reports, metrics, insights
  • Support your customers — troubleshooting, documentation

Getting Started

  1. Connect your accounts — Link your email, calendar, and other tools
  2. Set your preferences — Tell me how you like to work
  3. Ask me anything — "What's on my calendar today?" or "Help me draft an email"

Example Conversations

User: "I'm new here, what can you do?" Myah: "Welcome! I'm Myah, your AI assistant. I can help you manage emails, schedule meetings, organize documents, analyze data, and much more. What would you like to explore first?"

User: "How do I get my calendar set up?" Myah: "I can help you connect your calendar and show you how to manage your schedule. Would you like me to walk you through the setup process?"

Tips for Success

  • Be specific in your requests
  • I learn from our conversations
  • You can install additional skills from the marketplace
  • Your data is private and secure

frontmatter.name: receipt-organizer
frontmatter.description: Extract data from receipts, categorize expenses, and generate expense reports

marketplace.summary: Extract receipt data and organize expenses automatically

receipt-organizer.description_html: Extract data from receipts, categorize expenses, and generate expense reports
receipt-organizer.body_html:
Receipt Organizer

Extract data from receipts, categorize expenses, and generate expense reports with minimal effort.

Features

  • Receipt parsing — Extract merchant, date, amount, and items from receipt text or images
  • Auto-categorization — Sort expenses into categories (meals, travel, office supplies, etc.)
  • Expense reports — Generate formatted reports for accounting or reimbursement
  • Mileage tracking — Log business trips with distance calculations
  • Receipt storage — Organize and archive receipts for tax purposes

How to Use

Share receipt details (text, photo description, or forwarded email) and I'll extract and organize the information.

Example Conversation

User: "I have a receipt from lunch with a client. It's from Bistro 42, dated April 15th, total was $67.50 including tip." Myah: "Got it. Here's the extracted receipt data:

Receipt Summary:

  • Merchant: Bistro 42
  • Date: April 15, 2026
  • Amount: $67.50
  • Category: Meals & Entertainment (client meeting)
  • Payment: [Not specified — please add if needed]

Expense Report Entry: | Date | Merchant | Category | Amount | Notes | |------|----------|----------|--------|-------| | 04/15/26 | Bistro 42 | Meals & Entertainment | $67.50 | Client lunch |

Would you like me to:

  1. Add this to your monthly expense report?
  2. Flag it for reimbursement?
  3. Categorize it differently?"

User: "Add it to my April expense report" Myah: "Added to your April 2026 expense report. Your current totals:

April 2026 Expenses:

  • Meals & Entertainment: $245.50 (3 receipts)
  • Travel: $120.00 (2 receipts)
  • Office Supplies: $45.99 (1 receipt)
  • Total: $411.49

You're within your $500 monthly budget. Would you like me to generate the full April report?"

User: "Yes, generate the report" Myah: "Here's your April 2026 expense report:

Expense Report — April 2026 Employee: [Your Name] Department: [Your Department] Report Date: April 22, 2026

Summary

  • Total Expenses: $411.49
  • Receipt Count: 6
  • Budget Status: Within limit ($500)

Breakdown by Category

  1. Meals & Entertainment — $245.50

    • Bistro 42 (04/15) — $67.50 — Client lunch
    • [Other receipts...]

  2. Travel — $120.00

    • [Receipt details...]

  3. Office Supplies — $45.99

    • [Receipt details...]

Reimbursement Request

Total requested: $411.49

Ready to submit? I can format this for your accounting system or email it to your manager."

Categories

  • Meals & Entertainment
  • Travel & Transportation
  • Office Supplies
  • Software & Tools
  • Professional Development
  • Marketing & Advertising
  • Miscellaneous

Tips

  • Share receipts as soon as possible to avoid losing them
  • Include context ("client dinner", "team lunch") for better categorization
  • Review auto-categorizations — they learn from your corrections
  • Export monthly reports before submitting for reimbursement

frontmatter.name: research-brief
frontmatter.description: Compile research findings into structured briefs with sources, key insights, and recommendations

marketplace.summary: Compile research into structured briefs with insights and sources

research-brief.description_html: Compile research findings into structured briefs with sources, key insights, and recommendations
research-brief.body_html:
Research Brief

Compile research findings into professional briefs with structured sections, source tracking, and actionable recommendations.

Features

  • Structured format — Executive summary, methodology, findings, recommendations
  • Source tracking — Organize and cite sources with URLs and notes
  • Key insights extraction — Identify the most important findings
  • Comparison tables — Side-by-side analysis of options or data points
  • Export options — Markdown, PDF-friendly format, or presentation slides

How to Use

Share your research notes, links, or raw findings. I'll help you organize them into a professional brief.

Example Conversation

User: "I need to compile a brief on competitor pricing strategies" Myah: "I'll help you create a structured research brief. To get started, could you share:

  1. Which competitors are you analyzing?
  2. What specific pricing aspects (subscription tiers, discounts, enterprise)?
  3. Do you have any initial findings or sources?

Or if you have notes ready, just paste them and I'll organize everything."

User: "Here are my notes... [shares research notes]" Myah: "Excellent research! Here's your structured brief:

Competitor Pricing Strategy Analysis

Date: April 22, 2026 Researcher: [Your Name] Scope: SaaS pricing models in the project management space

Executive Summary

Three main competitors were analyzed: Asana, Monday.com, and Notion. Key findings:

  • Freemium is standard — All three offer free tiers with feature limitations
  • Per-seat pricing dominates — Range: $8–$15/user/month for premium tiers
  • Enterprise pricing is opaque — All require sales contact for custom quotes
  • Annual discounts average 18% — Standard incentive for yearly commitments

Methodology

  • Sources: Company websites, G2 reviews, Capterra listings, press releases
  • Date range: Q1 2026 pricing data
  • Limitations: Enterprise pricing not publicly disclosed; based on estimates

Key Findings

1. Pricing Tier Structure

| Competitor | Free Tier | Basic | Pro | Enterprise | |------------|-----------|-------|-----|------------| | Asana | Yes | $10.99 | $24.99 | Custom | | Monday.com | Yes | $9 | $19 | Custom | | Notion | Yes | $8 | $15 | Custom |

2. Notable Strategies

  • Asana: Emphasizes workflow automation in higher tiers
  • Monday.com: Heavy focus on visual project tracking
  • Notion: Bundles docs, wikis, and projects — unique positioning

3. Discount Patterns

  • Annual billing: 15–20% discount
  • Non-profit: 50% discount (Asana, Monday.com)
  • Education: Free (all three)

Recommendations

  1. Match freemium expectation — Essential for market entry
  2. Price competitively at $10–$12/user — Sweet spot for basic tier
  3. Differentiate on features, not price — Avoid race to bottom
  4. Transparent enterprise pricing — Consider publishing ranges to reduce friction

Sources

  1. Asana Pricing — Retrieved April 2026
  2. Monday.com Pricing — Retrieved April 2026
  3. Notion Pricing — Retrieved April 2026
  4. G2 Grid Report: Project Management Software — Q1 2026

Would you like me to:

  • Add more competitors?
  • Create a comparison presentation?
  • Generate an executive one-pager?"

Brief Templates

  • Market Analysis — Competitors, trends, opportunities
  • Product Research — Features, user needs, technical requirements
  • User Research — Interviews, surveys, personas
  • Technical Research — Architecture, tools, feasibility
  • Business Case — ROI, costs, risks, recommendations

Tips

  • Start with a clear research question
  • Track sources as you go — it's hard to reconstruct later
  • Distinguish between facts and interpretations
  • Include limitations — honest research is more credible
  • Focus on actionable recommendations, not just findings
